html:
Versico Single-Ply Systems on Laredo Roofs

Versico is, like Carlisle SynTec, part of the Carlisle Companies building-products organization, operating as a related but separate brand with its own product lines and distribution approach. We spec Versico membrane on a portion of our jobs where their specific product or distributor availability fits the project better than an alternative manufacturer.

A Sister Company to Carlisle SynTec

Versico and Carlisle SynTec share a parent organization, which means there's real overlap in underlying manufacturing between the two brands, but they operate as distinct product lines sold through different channels. Understanding that relationship helps explain why some of Versico's product characteristics resemble Carlisle's while the brands are marketed and distributed separately, each with its own dealer network and its own warranty program administered independently of the other.

The VersiFlex and VersiWeld Product Lines

Versico markets its EPDM products under the VersiFlex name and its TPO and PVC single-ply lines under VersiWeld, alongside insulation, fastening, and coating products meant to round out a complete roofing assembly. The catalog breadth is comparable to what other major single-ply manufacturers offer - standard thickness options in TPO, EPDM, and PVC, sold as part of a documented system rather than membrane alone.

Versico's catalog beyond membrane includes polyisocyanurate insulation board, cover boards, fastening plates, and edge and termination metal designed to be specified as one matched system. As with any manufacturer on our list, we source these components together rather than mixing insulation from one supplier with membrane from another, since a mixed assembly can complicate a warranty claim later if a failure occurs at the boundary between two manufacturers' products.

Coatings and Recover Products

Versico also produces reflective coating products used in recover and recoat projects on existing roofs, similar in purpose to the coating work described on our silicone roof coating page. We evaluate their coating line against other manufacturers' options on the same basis as membrane - fit for the specific roof's condition and exposure, not brand loyalty.

Distributor Network Instead of Direct Sales

Versico has historically leaned on an independent distributor network for product sales rather than selling exclusively direct, which affects availability and lead times on certain products depending on regional distributor stock. We check current product availability before committing to a Versico spec on a job with a tight schedule, the same way we would with any manufacturer whose distribution model isn't direct-to-contractor.

  • Product line - VersiFlex vs. VersiWeld - matched to membrane type needed for the building
  • Current distributor availability checked before the spec is finalized for a tight-schedule job
  • Insulation and fastening components sourced as a matched system per specification
  • Field weld or seam work documented to the standard the warranty requires
  • Warranty registration completed promptly after project close-out

Warranty Structure on Versico Systems

Versico's warranty terms, like every manufacturer covered here, are specific to the product line, installation method, and project documentation. We review the actual warranty document for the specific system being installed with the owner directly, rather than describing coverage in general terms that might not match the specific product or project.

Where Versico Comes Up on Laredo Jobs

We tend to reach for Versico on jobs where distributor stock or lead time on a specific membrane thickness works out better than a competing manufacturer's at the time of the project, or where an owner already has other Versico-roofed buildings in a portfolio and wants to keep specifications consistent across properties. It's rarely the deciding factor on its own; it's one input among several, alongside cost, availability, and the building's specific exposure profile.

Because the two brands share manufacturing roots, an owner comparing a Versico VersiWeld quote against a Carlisle TPO quote is often comparing two products with more in common than the different names and marketing suggest. Where the real differences show up are current pricing, which distributor's stock is closer and faster for this specific job, and occasionally a small difference in accessory product design between the two catalogs. We walk owners through those specific differences rather than treating the brand names as interchangeable or as fundamentally different products.

Versico's own catalog beyond membrane includes polyisocyanurate insulation, cover boards, and fastening and edge-metal accessories meant to be specified alongside the membrane as one documented system, similar in scope to what Carlisle offers under its own name.

Questions About Versico Roofing Systems

Is Versico the same as Carlisle SynTec?

They share a parent company, Carlisle Companies, but operate as separate brands with distinct product lines and distribution channels.

What's the difference between VersiFlex and VersiWeld?

VersiFlex is Versico's EPDM product line, available in both black and white formulations; VersiWeld covers their TPO and PVC single-ply membranes across the standard thickness range.

Does Versico sell direct to contractors?

Versico has historically relied on independent distributors rather than exclusively direct sales, which we account for when checking product availability on a tight-schedule job before it becomes a problem mid-project.

Why would you choose Versico over Carlisle SynTec for a job?

The decision usually comes down to specific product availability, distributor lead time, or a particular product characteristic that fits the building better at the time of the project, rather than any fundamental performance gap between the two sister brands.

What does a Versico warranty cover?

That depends on the specific product line and how the project is installed and documented; we review the actual terms with the owner before any contract is signed.
